title: aeluropus niliacus
intro: Aeluropus niliacus, also known as the desert grass or wild salt grass, is a species of grass that grows in coastal areas and salt-affected regions. The plant has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Aeluropus niliacus include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and soothe skin irritations. In terms of herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and roots can be used to make teas, infusions, and topical applications, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and potential side effects.
